Details: One study reported that chlortetracycline was effectively removed by an ion-exchange resin, MIEX, in a batch test [1527]. More than 90 percent of chlortetracycline was removed from de-ionized water using a resin dose ranging from 0.5 to 5.0 mL/L. A range of 65 to 95 percent removal was achieved from treated river water containing 0.8 mg/L DOC at the same resin dose range. Resin dose did not affect removal efficiency.
